summary: Parallel review: FAIL (4 critical, 11 warning, 5 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:1532` — Warning block guard `!simplifyResult.skipped` does not exclude `escalated` or `reverted` states; when `escalated: true` and `findings.warning > 0`, both the escalation progress entry (line 1519) and the warning progress entry (line 1539) are appended to `progress.md` for the same simplify run — same double-write occurs for `reverted + warnings`; add `&& !simplifyResult.escalated && !simplifyResult.reverted` to the guard

🟡 `test/simplify-pass.test.mjs:505` — Both new tests are source-text string-inclusion checks; they verify strings exist in source but do not test runtime behavior (file written when `findings.warning > 0`, or NOT written when `findings.warning === 0`) — carried from prior reviews

🟡 test/simplify-pass.test.mjs:505 — Source-text check `src.includes("simplify-eval.md")` does not verify the condition controlling when `writeFileSync` fires; mutations to the `> 0` threshold or `!skipped` guard are invisible; add a behavioral mock test with `findings: { warning: 2 }` that asserts the file is written

🟡 test/simplify-pass.test.mjs:516 — Non-blocking test verifies string presence only; adding `|| warning > 0` to the finalize guard would still pass this test; a behavioral test is needed to verify the gate stays clean when warnings > 0

🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:1535` — `simplify-eval.md` records only the warning count; actual warning text from agent output is discarded — add the raw finding lines so users can act on them without re-running the pass

🟡 `test/simplify-pass.test.mjs:505` — warning-reporting tests are source-text pattern checks only; a behavioral test that invokes the warning path and asserts `simplify-eval.md` is written with correct content would provide stronger protection
The core behavior is correct — warnings are surfaced to both files and do not block. The two 🟡 findings go to backlog: the actionability of `simplify-eval.md` content, and test coverage depth.

🟡 `test/simplify-pass.test.mjs:505` — Both task-4 tests are source-text assertions; they verify strings exist in `run.mjs` but cannot catch: wrong condition expression, wrong file path, `appendProgress` removed, or a regression adding a warning check to the finalize gate — add a behavioral test that drives the warning path with `findings: { warning: 2 }` and asserts file creation and progress append

🟡 `test/simplify-pass.test.mjs:519` — `assert.ok(finalizeBlockIdx !== -1, "finalize gate must only check escalated, not warnings")` does not verify its stated claim; it proves `simplifyResult?.escalated` exists somewhere in source — a `|| warnings > 0` regression in the finalize guard at run.mjs:1548 would still pass this test undetected

**Summary:** The implementation is correct — warnings write to `simplify-eval.md` and `progress.md`, finalize is not blocked. The two 🟡 findings continue the source-text-assertion coverage gap pattern flagged throughout prior tasks, plus the specific new problem that the "non-blocking" test cannot actually detect a finalize regression. No 🔴 blockers. Eval written to `tasks/task-4/eval.md`.
🟡 bin/lib/run.mjs:1627 — `activePhases2` duplicates `activePhases` (line 1601); same computation from the same array literal in the same scope — reuse the earlier variable

- `activePhases2` at `run.mjs:1627` recomputes an identical filter over the same array that was already computed as `activePhases` at line 1601. Both are in scope through the end of the function. The `2` suffix is a smell. Filed as 🟡 (not dead code — it is used — so not a 🔴 veto).

- `runSimplifyFixLoop` has one production call site, which would mechanically trigger the premature-abstraction veto. However, it encapsulates 14 lines of non-trivial loop logic and is the only reason the fix-loop behavior is testable without running actual git operations. The anti-pattern rule ("don't demand simplicity at the cost of correctness") applies — not flagged 🔴.
